summary.case_definition: WHO 2026: confirmed (RT-PCR or IgM seroconversion), probable (clinical + epi link), suspected (clinical only)

countries.name: Argentina
countries.notes: Approximately 2x baseline rate since June 2025. Endemic region for ANDV.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.notes: Dutch couple infected during MV Hondius shore excursion in Argentina. Both deceased.

countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: German national, MV Hondius passenger. Deceased.

countries.name: United States
countries.notes: Sporadic cases in southwestern states. 41 people under CDC monitoring: 16 at Nebraska NQU, 2 at Emory (Atlanta), 7 former passengers already home, plus flight contacts. No Americans currently positive for hantavirus (Dr. Kornfeld's inconclusive test confirmed negative May 13). Quarantine voluntary — no federal or state orders. Illinois investigating separate potential case in Winnebago County (rodent exposure, NOT linked to cruise).

countries.name: Spain
countries.notes: One confirmed case: Spanish male passenger from MV Hondius, asymptomatic, detected upon arrival at Gómez Ulla Hospital UATAN isolation unit in Madrid (announced May 12). 13 other Spanish evacuees tested negative. Alicante flight-contact (32-year-old woman) PCR tests negative so far — reclassified as contact under monitoring, allowed visitors pending repeat negative PCR. Three flight-linked contacts (Alicante, Barcelona, Catalonia) all under monitoring, none confirmed. Sources: Al Jazeera, Infobae, Democrata (May 12–14 2026).

countries.name: Singapore
countries.notes: Two residents isolated at NCID — 67-year-old male (asymptomatic) and 65-year-old male (mild rhinorrhoea). Both tested negative; completing 30-day quarantine. No confirmed cases.

countries.name: Canada
countries.notes: One confirmed case: Yukon resident, MV Hondius passenger, presumptive positive May 16, confirmed May 17 by PHAC National Microbiology Lab in Winnipeg. Hospitalized in isolation in British Columbia with spouse. 36 Canadians connected to outbreak total. Flight ban for MV Hondius passenger/crew entering Canada remains in effect. Sources: CBC, PHAC (May 16-17 2026).

countries.name: Cape Verde
countries.notes: MV Hondius was quarantined at Praia May 1-6 before departing for Tenerife. No cases. Monitoring complete.

countries.name: Finland
countries.notes: Endemic Puumala virus cases, typical seasonal pattern.

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.notes: Swiss male MV Hondius passenger confirmed by Swiss Federal Office of Public Health on May 6-7. Receiving treatment at University Hospital Zurich.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.notes: Two British nationals confirmed: Martin Anstee (case 5, expedition guide) hospitalised in the Netherlands; second British national hospitalised in South Africa. Third British national with suspected hantavirus on Tristan da Cunha (disembarked en route). 22 UK returnees completed 72-hour quarantine at Arrowe Park Hospital (Merseyside), now self-isolating at home for 42 days with daily UKHSA contact. Sources: UKHSA GOV.UK, ITV News, STV (May 12-13 2026).

countries.name: France
countries.notes: One confirmed case: French female MV Hondius passenger, became symptomatic during repatriation, now in intensive care in France — condition deteriorating per Health Minister Stephanie Rist. France tracking 26 close contacts from shared flights; all have tested negative. Previous flight-contact case (French man, Saint Helena–Johannesburg) remains under monitoring, asymptomatic. Sources: Al Jazeera, WHO DON601 (May 12–13 2026).

countries.name: South Africa
countries.notes: One British MV Hondius passenger confirmed with hantavirus and hospitalised in South Africa (May 8 2026). Ship's doctor also evacuated to Johannesburg ICU. Sources: UKHSA GOV.UK, Al Jazeera, RTE (May 8 2026).
